name: TDD-STATE-MACHINE-BUG-2
title: HUMAN-APPROVAL gate silently skipped — TDD subagent cannot interactively pause; gate must move to dispatcher
description:
The TDD subagent prompt includes Step 2 (HUMAN-APPROVAL): 'invoke writing-plans, present summary to the user, wait for explicit approval before Step 3 (LOCK).' This gate is silently skipped on every subagent dispatch.

Root cause: the Agent tool dispatches a subagent that runs to completion and returns one result. There is no pause-and-resume mechanism. A subagent cannot mid-execution surface output to the user, block on input, and then continue. The subagent reads the HUMAN-APPROVAL instruction, has no user to present to, and proceeds to LOCK without any pause.

This is not an Anthropic API limitation to work around — it is a design error in the TDD state machine. The gate was placed inside the subagent where it can never fire. It must be moved to the dispatcher (main session / rabbit-feature-touch Step 3.5) where interactive user communication is possible.

Required redesign:
1. Remove HUMAN-APPROVAL from the TDD subagent's 9-step sequence. Subagent starts at LOCK (Step 3), not SPEC-READ (Step 1) — or subagent still reads spec and produces a plan artifact, but does NOT proceed past plan production without external signal.
2. Add a dispatcher-side HUMAN-APPROVAL gate in rabbit-feature-touch between Step 3 (Spec Authoring) and Step 4 (Dispatch TDD Subagents). After rabbit-spec completes, the dispatcher reads the impl-suggestion, presents a summary to the user, and waits for explicit approval before calling dispatch-tdd-subagent.py.
3. The bypass flag (--no-human-approval) is passed to dispatch-tdd-subagent.py to signal the subagent it should proceed directly to LOCK without generating a plan — not to skip a gate that does not exist.
4. Any other step that requires an interactive pause (e.g., mid-cycle user checkpoints) must follow the same pattern: the pause-point is owned by the dispatcher or main session, not embedded inside a subagent step.

This is the general architectural principle: subagents are fire-and-forget units. All human-in-the-loop gates must live in the session layer that can actually talk to the user.

Related: TDD-SUBAGENT-BACKLOG-1 (--no-human-approval switch has no enforcement test). TDD-STATE-MACHINE-BACKLOG-1 (human review gate for spec + plan). RABBIT-CAGE-BUG-6 fix session exposed this gap (2026-05-17) — subagent ran LOCK→TEST-WRITE→IMPLEMENT without any user gate.

history.note: Architectural fix already in place: dispatcher-level HUMAN-APPROVAL gate exists in rabbit-feature-touch SKILL.md Step 4 and is documented in spec Invariant 14. Subagent Step 2 is now dead code in non-autonomous mode but functionally harmless — user approval fires at dispatcher before subagent dispatch. Remaining dead-code cleanup tracked in new backlog item.
